summary: Session 2 (last) of 103-copilot-ado-hello-world-tutorial: operator-conducted live UAT validation walk + de-draft. The operator ran the Session-2 live walk against the set's UAT checklist on a real Azure DevOps org + GitHub Copilot seat (2026-07-15) and marked all 11 functional-area walks Passes: true (Copilot seat CLI, ADO bootstrap, build+seat+auth-preflight, modules+branch policies, the Set-102-armed Open PR, the Copilot-driven session, the first-ever live azure-pipelines.yml run, the Set-102-armed Finalize, integration+touches review, tag/hotfix/rollback drills, no-CLI floor), attesting the flow passed. On that basis: (1) de-drafted docs/tutorials/module-team-hello-world-copilot-ado.md (PREVIEW banner -> validated end-to-end on a live ADO org + Copilot seat, 2026-07-15); (2) activated the preview cross-links in the base tutorial (2 spots), quick-start, and the extension README (README Marketplace visibility rides the next publish -- no extension release for this docs-only set); (3) recorded s2-uat-attestation.md with the operator's verbatim attestation + the per-item pass record; (4) discharged Set 102's armed Azure DevOps UAT (Walks 5 & 8, cross-referenced; 102 stays immutable). VERIFICATION: round-1 discovery (gpt-5-6, 2/2 fan-out) found 4 Major from one root cause -- the UAT checklist carried 11 blank duplicate rows (Passes: false, empty) appended by the UAT tool alongside the 11 real Passes: true walks, making it read as contradictory and the claims unsupported. Remediated: removed the 11 blank rows (Review 22->11, all Passes: true), corrected the attestation to reflect the real per-item passes (Result/Feedback left blank per the ad-hoc floor), bounded the de-draft claims to the walk record. Round-2 remediation-review (gpt-5-6): VERIFIED, 3 fixes accepted, 0 blocking. The close backstop (round 3, full-diff) then re-flagged one Major: the public 'validated'/102-discharge claims are stronger than the DOCUMENTED per-item evidence (each walk carries the operator's Passes: true mark, but the free-text Result/Feedback narrative is blank). This is unresolvable by a routed model -- the acceptance test is a live operator walk the model did not witness. The operator, the acceptance authority for this requiresUAT set, conducted that walk, attests it passed ('I verify that 103 is good as is. I just completed the UAT'), and directed the de-draft; on that authority the finding is WAIVED and the set closed via the attested --manual-verify override (s2-uat-attestation.md). Honest framing: operator-attested acceptance with per-item pass-marks but no per-item narrative -- not a routed clean verification, not confabulated. No code change (docs-only set); no Marketplace release (per spec: no release boundary).
